commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2e695a9004b49e8856e915c3d5e9849c9b6aa8dd)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds provider-plugin structured error classification hooks/runtime dispatch, propagates status/code/errorType through failover classification, and adds focused provider/error tests.

PR surface: Source +370, Tests +521. Total +891 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR rather than a bug report. The submitted proof is not high-confidence for the current head because its command imports a provider-errors SDK file that is absent from the branch.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is author/maintainer follow-up on the public provider-error API contract and refreshed real behavior proof, not a safe repair-only lane while proof is inconsistent.

target.title: feat: add structured provider error descriptors
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a backwards-compatible structured provider-error contract so provider plugins can classify stable upstream errors without putting provider-specific policy in core.

- adds `ProviderErrorDescriptor` / `ProviderErrorClassification` and a preferred `classifyProviderError(ctx)` hook while keeping existing `classifyFailoverReason(ctx)` support
- adds the public SDK subpath `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-errors` with `defineProviderErrorMap(...)`, code/type normalization, structured payload extraction, recovery metadata, and false-positive protection for prose matches
- routes provider hook results through the existing failover code/status ordering, including provider scoping, providerless legacy fallback behavior, context-overflow handling, and ambiguous 401/402/403 refinement
- keeps the hot error-helper import path light by putting provider-error dispatch behind `provider-error-runtime`, with a lazy default resolver for error-helper-only call paths
- documents the current legacy flow, the structured flow, backwards-compatibility guidance, hook ordering, and generic example provider mappings with diagrams
- keeps examples provider-neutral (`demo-provider` with `PROVIDER_QUOTA_EXHAUSTED`), with no bundled-provider-specific mappings or `extensions/xai` changes

## Why

Provider plugins know their own stable upstream error codes, statuses, and recovery hints. OpenClaw core should not grow provider-specific branches for those cases, but it does need a generic contract so plugins can surface better failover reasons and, later, better UI/channel recovery metadata.

This PR keeps the architecture provider-neutral: plugin authors can return a structured descriptor from their provider plugin, core consumes the generic `reason` today, and future surfaces can render typed metadata such as `userMessage`, `action`, and `retryAfterMs` without changing the plugin API again.

This is intentionally additive. Existing plugins that only implement `classifyFailoverReason` continue to work, and new plugins can opt into `classifyProviderError` when they have stable provider-owned signals.

## Current flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  request["Provider request fails"] --> extract["Core extracts error text"]
  extract --> overflow["matchesContextOverflowError?"]
  extract --> legacy["classifyFailoverReason(ctx)"]
  legacy --> reason["FailoverReason string"]
  reason --> copy["Generic failover and user error copy"]
```

## New flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  request["Provider request fails"] --> extract["Core extracts message, status, code, and errorType"]
  extract --> overflow["Context-overflow classification stays first-class"]
  extract --> structured["classifyProviderError(ctx)"]
  structured --> descriptor["ProviderErrorDescriptor"]
  descriptor --> reason["reason drives failover and generic copy"]
  descriptor --> metadata["userMessage, action, retryAfterMs for UI surfaces"]
  structured --> fallback["No match"]
  fallback --> legacy["classifyFailoverReason(ctx)"]
  legacy --> generic["Generic classifiers"]
```

## Compatibility notes

- `classifyProviderError` is preferred for new plugins, but `classifyFailoverReason` remains valid and is still called as fallback.
- Provider hooks are scoped by provider id when a provider is known, so unrelated provider hooks cannot classify each other's errors.
- Providerless raw error classification keeps the legacy fallback path, while structured status/code signals without provider attribution keep conservative generic status behavior.
- Context-overflow messages remain first-class for compaction/retry behavior, but stable provider fields can still disambiguate misleading overflow-looking prose.
- Code/status precedence is preserved for unambiguous statuses; provider descriptors can refine ambiguous 401/402/403 responses before the generic auth/billing fallback.
- `codes` map entries match direct `ctx.code` / `ctx.errorType` or explicit structured `code:` / `type:` fields in provider payload text. Plain prose matching must use `messagePatterns`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: a provider plugin can surface a stable upstream usage-cap code through the generic structured provider-error hook, and the shared failover classifier preserves provider scoping and status precedence.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on branch `codex/provider-error-descriptors`, rebased onto current `origin/main`, Node 24.14.0, with a synthetic provider registered into an active OpenClaw plugin registry through the real runtime registry path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx - <<'PROOF'
import { classifyFailoverSignal } from "./src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/errors.js";
import { defineProviderErrorMap } from "./src/plugin-sdk/provider-errors.js";
import { classifyProviderErrorWithPlugin } from "./src/plugins/provider-error-runtime.js";
import { createEmptyPluginRegistry } from "./src/plugins/registry.js";
import { resetPluginRuntimeStateForTest, setActivePluginRegistry } from "./src/plugins/runtime.js";

resetPluginRuntimeStateForTest();
const classifyKnownProviderError = defineProviderErrorMap([
  {
    codes: ["PROVIDER_QUOTA_EXHAUSTED"],
    status: [402, 403],
    reason: "billing",
    userMessage: "Your provider quota has been exhausted.",
    action: {
      kind: "usage",
      label: "Review usage",
      url: "https://provider.example.com/usage",
    },
  },
]);
const registry = createEmptyPluginRegistry();
registry.providers.push({
  pluginId: "demo-provider",
  pluginName: "Demo Provider",
  source: "provider-error-proof",
  provider: {
    id: "demo-provider",
    label: "Demo Provider",
    auth: [],
    classifyProviderError: classifyKnownProviderError,
  },
});
setActivePluginRegistry(registry, "provider-error-proof", "default", process.cwd());
const quotaExhausted403 = {
  provider: "demo-provider",
  errorMessage: "Forbidden",
  status: 403,
  code: "PROVIDER_QUOTA_EXHAUSTED",
};
console.log(JSON.stringify({
  registeredProviderIds: registry.providers.map((entry) => entry.provider.id),
  descriptor403: classifyProviderErrorWithPlugin({
    provider: "demo-provider",
    context: quotaExhausted403,
  }),
  failover403: classifyFailoverSignal({
    provider: "demo-provider",
    message: "Forbidden",
    status: 403,
    code: "PROVIDER_QUOTA_EXHAUSTED",
  }),
  failover429: classifyFailoverSignal({
    provider: "demo-provider",
    message: "Forbidden",
    status: 429,
    code: "PROVIDER_QUOTA_EXHAUSTED",
  }),
  providerScopeCheck: classifyFailoverSignal({
    provider: "other-provider",
    message: "Forbidden",
    status: 403,
    code: "PROVIDER_QUOTA_EXHAUSTED",
  }),
}, null, 2));
resetPluginRuntimeStateForTest();
PROOF
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "registeredProviderIds": [
    "demo-provider"
  ],
  "descriptor403": {
    "reason": "billing",
    "status": 403,
    "code": "PROVIDER_QUOTA_EXHAUSTED",
    "userMessage": "Your provider quota has been exhausted.",
    "action": {
      "kind": "usage",
      "label": "Review usage",
      "url": "https://provider.example.com/usage"
    }
  },
  "failover403": {
    "kind": "reason",
    "reason": "billing"
  },
  "failover429": {
    "kind": "reason",
    "reason": "rate_limit"
  },
  "providerScopeCheck": {
    "kind": "reason",
    "reason": "auth"
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: The active demo provider returned a structured billing descriptor, 403 `PROVIDER_QUOTA_EXHAUSTED` resolved to billing, 429 still resolved to rate-limit, and the same code scoped to another provider stayed generic auth instead of borrowing the demo provider's policy.

What was not tested: Live upstream account-limit responses and UI rendering of descriptor metadata. This PR adds the SDK/runtime contract; a later UI pass can render `userMessage` and `action` directly.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof uses a local provider-plugin registration and synthetic upstream error fields so no real account credentials or usage-cap account state are required.

## Verification

- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check`
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs README.md`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`
- `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/provider-errors.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/errors-provider-runtime-integration.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/errors-provider-hook-order.test.ts src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/provider-error-patterns.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.isbillingerrormessage.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-error-runtime.test.ts extensions/xai/index.test.ts extensions/amazon-bedrock/index.test.ts` -> 13 files, 420 tests passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts` -> 1 file, 17 tests passed
- `base=20 20 12 61 79 80 81 701 33 98 100 204 250 395 398 399 400git merge-base HEAD origin/main) && git diff "" --name-only | rg '^extensions/xai/'` -> no matches; added-diff scan for provider-specific placeholder remnants -> no matches
- `pnpm build`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings reported
target.author: fuller-stack-dev
